```json
[
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Network Expansion Velocity Surge",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region through openchargemap adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rapid week-over-week increases in public EV charger installations signal acceleration in green infrastructure capex, indicating bullish macro stimulus or corporate EV investment push.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Industrial sector benefits from infrastructure spending and EV supply chain acceleration."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly charger count increase exceeds 3% week-over-week and 7-day moving average is positive",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or if weekly growth falls below 1%",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "EV charger networks expand consistently; 3% weekly growth occurs multiple times per quarter in most regions."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake Activity Reinsurance Volatility Spike",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake API magnitude 4.5+ events by week through earthquake_activity adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Clustering of moderate-to-strong earthquakes in populated zones triggers rapid repricing of catastrophe reinsurance and property-linked derivatives, creating short-term financial sector rotation.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Insurance and reinsurance stocks face claim reserve repricing; financial sector sentiment shifts on tail-risk realization."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within a 7-day rolling window in the continental US or California, short XLF",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when weekly earthquake count drops to 0",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "California and Western US experience earthquake clusters regularly; 3+ events in a week occurs 2–4 times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Google Trends Surge in Flight Delay Complaints",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'flight delays' + 'airline cancellation' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp spikes in Google search volume for flight delay complaints signal operational stress in airlines and airports, often preceding earnings pressure or guidance cuts.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary travel sentiment deteriorates; airlines and leisure stocks face margin and demand headwinds."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Google Trends index for 'flight delays' exceeds 70 (out of 100) and rises 15+ points from prior week",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when index falls below 55",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal airline disruptions, weather events, and staffing crises drive this signal weekly in peak travel seasons; fires at least monthly year-round."
  },
  {
    "idea": "FRED Jobless Claims Volatility Pop Reversion",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ICSA (Initial Jobless Claims) weekly through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When initial jobless claims spike 20% week-over-week (often noise-driven or seasonal), they often revert sharply the following week, creating mean-reversion trading opportunity in defensive and cyclical sectors.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Extreme jobless claim spikes are often false signals or seasonal; mean reversion favors cyclical consumer stocks."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly ICSA increases >20% from prior week, enter long XLY at close",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days or if ICSA falls week-over-week",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal and holiday-related jobless claim spikes occur predictably; >20% moves happen 3–5 times annually."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Port Container Velocity Slowdown Liquidation",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Port container volume (TEU) via port_container_volume adapter for top 5 US ports, weekly",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When weekly port container volumes drop >15% from the prior week (signaling demand shock or inventory flush), logistics and industrial stocks face sharp repricing.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Container volume collapse signals upstream demand destruction, freight rate pressure, and logistics capex cuts."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If aggregate 5-port TEU volume drops >15% week-over-week, short XLI",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when weekly volume rebounds >5%",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Post-holiday liquidation, seasonal demand shifts, and supply-chain corrections produce >15% weekly swings 2–4 times annually."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Temperature Shock Utility Bill Anxiety Google Spike",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly 'electricity bill shock' + 'high utility costs' through google_trends adapter, paired with Open-Meteo daily max temps for regional anomaly",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Extreme heat or cold events trigger simultaneous Google Trends spikes in utility cost anxiety searches, signaling consumer stress pickup and household budget pressure.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Elevated utility bill anxiety dampens discretionary spending; consumer goods and leisure stocks face demand leakage."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Trends index for utility bill keywords >65 AND regional temp anomaly >+8°F or <-8°F from 30-year normal, short XLY",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when Trends index falls below 50",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal temperature extremes (summer heat, winter cold) occur reliably; anxiety spikes fire monthly in most years."
  },
  {
    "idea": "News RSS Surge on Semiconductor Supply Bottleneck",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed article count (via rss_count adapter) filtering for 'semiconductor shortage' + 'chip supply' from major tech/finance outlets, daily",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in news article volume about semiconductor supply constraints correlate with short-term panic selling in tech and industrial sectors, creating oversold rebound opportunities.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Extreme attention to chip shortages often precedes capitulation and technical rebound in semiconductor-exposed tech."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS article count on semiconductor bottleneck exceeds 50 articles and is 2 standard deviations above 30-day mean",
      "exit": "After 6 trading days or if article count falls below 20",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Supply chain disruption narratives resurge monthly; 50+ articles in a day fires multiple times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "BTS Airline Load Factor Capacity Stress Indicator",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) airline load factor (passenger utilization %) weekly through bts_airline_load_factor adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When airline load factors exceed 88% and rise >2 percentage points week-over-week, it signals capacity stress and pricing power, supporting airline stock but stressing seat availability and customer satisfaction.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "High load factors indicate pricing strength and full planes; airlines benefit from margin expansion."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly load factor >88% AND increases >2 pts from prior week, long XLY",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or if load factor falls below 86%",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Peak travel seasons and capacity constraints drive load factors >88% regularly; fires 4–6 times annually."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EIA Electricity Demand Shock Rebound Spread",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "EIA weekly electricity demand (net generation MWh) through eia_electricity adapter, tracked regionally",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp week-over-week drops in electricity demand (>8%) often indicate demand shock or recession fears, which revert quickly as underlying activity resumes or weather normalizes.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Demand collapses are often temporary; utilities rebound as demand normalizes, and utility stocks offer defensive stability."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly electricity demand drops >8% from prior week, long XLU",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or if demand recovers >4%",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal demand swings, weather volatility, and demand shocks produce >8% weekly moves 3–5 times annually."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Price Momentum Reversion in Defensive Sectors After Tech Crash",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily closing prices for XLK (tech) and XLV (healthcare) through price_only adapter, computing 5-day rolling returns",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When XLK underperforms XLV by >4% over 5 consecutive trading days, it often signals fear-driven rotation into defensive healthcare; subsequent mean reversion favors tech outperformance.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Extreme defensive rotations are often overdone; tech rebounds as rotation exhausts."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 5-day cumulative return for XLK < XLV by >4%, long XLK relative to XLV",
      "exit": "After 6 trading days or if relative performance reverses >2%",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Daily volatility and sector rotation create 4%+ relative moves weekly; signal fires multiple times per month."
  }
]
```
